verdict_reasoning: IZUMO4 is a soluble protein without a transmembrane domain, predicted to be intra-acrosomal in sperm. Despite being part of the IZUMO family involved in fertilization, it lacks any mechanism to anchor itself to the outer leaflet of the plasma membrane. Immunofluorescence studies failed to detect IZUMO4 on the sperm surface, and the protein is cytoplasmic/intra-acrosomal by localization. No evidence supports surface accessibility via TM domain, GPI anchor, or stable complex partnership with a surface protein.
